---
title: "Serving"
format: html # This qmd file will be compiled as an HTML web page
---

# Model serving (API)

<details class="austria">
<summary class="austria-header">
  <span class="solutionbox-icon"></span>
  austria
</summary>

<div class="solutionbox-body">
Since all our models are implemented in R, we use plumber APIs to deploy our models on RSConnect. 

</div>
</details>

# Deployment

<details class="destatis">
<summary class="destatis-header">
  <span class="solutionbox-icon"></span>
  Practical experience: deployment at Destatis
</summary>

<div class="solutionbox-body">

- Using separate environments for development and production, that are build using the same set-up for an easy deployment using CI/CD workflows (for instance with Gitlab). 
- Using Cloudera AI Model component to standardize and simplify the transition from experimentation to production. The model component package a trained model (from experiments or registry), define how it should be served, deploy it as a live endpoint, monitor/version it and roll back if needed. Cloudera builds a Docker container image automatically, including a base runtime (Python, R, etc.), dependencies needed, model and serving code (wrapper), that will be deployed on a Kubernetes Cluster (managed by Cloudera, but possibly hosted on your private servers). At the end of the process, the model is exposed as a REST API endpoint, using the wrapper function as explained in chapter 2.  

</div>
</details>

<details class="austria">
<summary class="austria-header">
  <span class="solutionbox-icon"></span>
  austria
</summary>

<div class="solutionbox-body">
An R package specifically for the process of querying of the models deployed through APIs was created. The input data, and other hyperparameters like code, are sent as json format to the API, and results are collected. This is done using the httr package. The output is then transformed into an output table and can be stored locally in any format.
</div>
</details>

# From dev to production: deal with environments
<details class="austria">
<summary class="austria-header">
  <span class="solutionbox-icon"></span>
  Statistics Austria:
</summary>

<div class="solutionbox-body">
Package versions are specified in a requirements.txt to make sure that all package versions on the developement server are synchronised with the packages used on the RSConnect instance.
</div>
</details>
